R:HSE:11:2026-07-09.comment: 3. THREE EYED CANDY went back early from his wide draw over 366m in stakes class at this track (Dirt) last time but hit the line strongly to finish fourth. Look for another barnstorming finish. 5. FAV STREAKIN CARTEL was beaten just 0.1 lengths last start and landed his first top-three finish in the process at this track (Dirt) over 320m. Looks close to a win. 4. MADAM MIM was prominent from the outset last time and held on well for third over 274m at this track (Dirt). Should be ready to show her best now so she is a strong chance to break through. 8. JOHN D was narrowly beaten into second when second up last start at this track (Dirt) over 320m. Will drift back but wind up late. Will be doing his best work late.

runners.name: THREE EYED CANDY
runners.comment: Third-up today. Resuming 4th of 10 at this track on May 30 over 366m, 1.0 len behind Sydney Jack carrying 57kg at $9.10. Second run from a spell 4th of 10 at this track in LR on June 18 over 366m, 2 len behind Tw Streakin Cowboy with 57kg at $75.90. Should be near his top.

runners.name: FAV STREAKIN CARTE
runners.comment: Third-up today. Resumes off a 58 day letup. First-up got back early 5th of 10 at this track on April 8 over 274m, 2.5 len behind Send Me A Dynasty Hg with 57kg at $24.40. Second run from a spell 2nd of 10 at this track on May 11 over 320m, 0.1 len behind Run Dont Walk with 57kg at $14.20. Fitter now and making headway. Will take beating.

runners.name: MADAM MIM
runners.comment: At the latest run raced on the pace when 3rd of 10 at this track on June 13 over 274m, 0.8 len behind Philomina with 57kg at $5.30. The race before that tracked the speed and boxed on steadily 5th of 9 at this track on May 11 over 320m, 1.75 len behind Jess Sinful carrying 57kg at $10. Another bold run is expected.

runners.name: JOHN D
runners.comment: Third-up today. Resuming eased back early on 8th of 10 at this track on May 30 over 366m, 5 len behind Roses After Midnight carrying 57kg at $9.70. Last start 2nd of 10 at this track on June 17 over 320m, 0.5 len behind Flying Knockout 123 with 58kg at $2.20. Swooper to watch for late in the piece.

runners.name: WANNA SWEET TREET
runners.comment: Comes here off a 39-day let-up. Most recently held ground to the line 3rd of 10 at this track on May 30 over 302m, 1.3 len behind Valiant Beacher carrying 58kg at $14.40. runners.name: S C SOTOL

runners.name: DOUG'S JAXX
runners.comment: Didn't do much at the last run, 8th (Fr6) over 1609m at this track beaten 25 m by Union Station (1:56.10) in NW1500L4 on June 28 at $5.80. Before that 6th (Fr4) over 1609m at this track, beaten 24 m by U Going (1:54.20) in NW2500L4 on June 14 at $62.20. Might find this too tough.

H:HOO:5:2026-07-09.comment: 5. JUST A START finished eighth last start over 1609m at this track, beaten 11 metres, after filling a minor placing at the previous appearance over 1609m at this track. Good enough to take out a race like this. 7. THOSESUMMERNIGHTS finished seventh at Northfield Park two runs ago before finishing seventh at her latest appearance over 1609m at Northfield Park, beaten 18 metres. Interesting to see what unfolds from this wide draw. Finds a suitable assignment and can bounce back to form. 6. runners.comment: Last start 4th (Fr5) over 1609m at this track beaten 7 m by Youremyobsession (1:54.00) in TMR?70 on June 23 at $3.20. Two runs back 5th (Fr2) over 1609m at this track, beaten 18 m by Knights Templar (1:53.30) in TMR?70 on June 16 at $3.40. Impossible to tip with any confidence.

ref.race_name: BARRY & BETTY HARVEY 50 YEARS IN RACING

R:CBG:1:2026-07-09.comment: 1. FORCE OF LAW has been trying to win a race for some time, and ran an encouraging third here just over 7 weeks ago when making his poly track debut. The gap between runs is the only concern, but with Michael McNab in the saddle, he will be hard to beat. 4. BIT OF A DAGG was only fair on debut at the end of January after some solid trial form. He was third in a trial some time ago, and if he doesn't need the run, then he can be a quick improver. Follow the market moves. 5. ALEZANDO was only beaten by a short-head here just over 3 weeks ago, and she won't have to improve by much to go one better. 2. REQUISITE has been thereabouts all prep, and he gets another attempt on this surface. In an even field, he is worth including in all top 4 combos.

runners.name: FORCE OF LAW
runners.comment: Comes here off a 50-day let-up. At the latest run came from midfield; 3rd of 11 at this track on May 20 over 1300m, 2.25 len behind Proud Capitalist with 55.5kg at $6.70. The run before that did the work up front then tired 5th of 14 at Woodville on April 29 over 1400m, slow going 1.2 len behind Timex with 58.5kg at $5.40. Looks one of the major contenders.

runners.name: REQUISITE
runners.comment: Last start sat on the speed and boxed on steadily 4th of 8 at Arawa Park on July 1 over 1215m, on a wet track; 2.75 len behind Echannay carrying 56.5kg at $3.20. Before that raced behind the speed prior to fading 8th of 11 at Tauranga on May 29 over 1400m, 4 len behind Miss Silhouette with 58.5kg at $3.90. Strong chance to break though.

runners.name: ALEZANDO
runners.trainer: G A & D Rogerson & C Isdale
runners.comment: Third-up today. Resuming 5th of 10 at this track on May 20 over 970m, 4.5 len behind Angels Brew with 56.5kg at $7.30. Last start raced on speed throughout when 2nd of 11 at this track on June 17 over 1300m, head behind Buccino with 56.5kg at $11. Fitter now and making headway. Will take plenty of beating.

runners.name: THREEZ COMPANY
runners.comment: At debut, 7th of 11 at Pukekohe on June 24 over 1200m, in the wet; 10 len behind Elite Choice with 55.5kg at $6.50. No surprise to see her in the finish.

runners.name: IN THE VINEYARD
runners.comment: Second-up. First-up after three months 4th of 10 at this track on June 17 over 970m, 3.5 len behind Smashing Sister with 53.5kg at $4.70. Previously second-up 2nd in a small field at New Plymouth Raceway on January 24 over 1200m, on rain affected going; 2.75 len behind King Of The Air with 54.5kg at $2.60. Rates highly and should be in the mix.

runners.name: BIT OF A DAGG
runners.comment: Resuming after a lengthy spell. Only one run when sat behind the speed prior to fading 8th of 10 at Te Rapa on January 30 over 1300m, on a slow track; 7 len behind Mosala carrying 57.5kg at $7.40. Looks all set for a positive return.

runners.name: BY DEFAULT
runners.comment: Resuming from a lengthy break. Only one run when 7th of 11 at New Plymouth Raceway Mdn on November 5 over 1600m, 6 len behind Shamwari with 56.5kg at $18.30. Others look better.

runners.name: ANNIE BLING
runners.comment: Last start 5th of 11 at this track on June 17 over 1300m, 3 len behind Buccino carrying 56.5kg at $30.60. The run before that raced on the pace before fading 7th of 12 at Te Aroha on June 1 over 1400m, on a slow track; 6 len behind Brewster with 55.5kg at $16.90. Promising signs last time and is worth including.

runners.name: LOMASI
runners.comment: At debut, 7th of 12 at Matamata on June 14 over 1400m, on a slow track; 5.5 len behind Dare To Proisir carrying 55.5kg at $11.80. Can do better. Plenty of scope for improvement.

runners.name: DARE YOU
runners.comment: Last start didn't do much in the run home; 8th of 10 at Te Aroha on June 21 over 1400m, on rain affected going; 11 len behind Sugar Daddy carrying 52.5kg at $15.80. Before that came from midfield; 2nd of 10 at Tauranga on May 29 over 1400m, 3 len behind Misissipi Gambler with 52.5kg at $35.30. Can improve on recent efforts.

runners.name: JUANITA
runners.comment: First starter by Vanbrugh out of Itsonlymakebelieve. Third May 26 at Te Awamutu at her only trial. Favouring others until she proves herself.

runners.name: MILLENNIUM MAGIC
runners.comment: She is resuming after a six-and-a-half months break. Finished off last campaign ninth of 13 at Arawa Park December 27 over 1400m, soft track; 8 len behind Timbuctoo with 54kg at $36.70. On debut ninth of 14 at Te Aroha December 7 over 1400m, 5 len behind Unwritten with 56.5kg at $44.40. Can't enthuse.

runners.name: TRIPLE THREAT
runners.comment: Third-up today. First-up 2nd of 8 at Hawera on May 17 over 1200m, on a slow track; 0.3 len behind Tongue In Cheek with 58.5kg at $5.40. Last start ran last of 9 at Te Aroha on June 21 over 1150m, in the wet; 11 len behind Winner Patch carrying 58.5kg at $3.50. Better than the last effort suggests and deserves another chance.

runners.name: TREASURE HUNT
runners.comment: She is resuming after a three-and-a-half months break. Not seen since she finished last of 12 at Matamata F&M Mdn-Sw March 22 over 1400m, soft track; 11 len behind Artamos with 55kg at $34.90. On debut ninth of 11 at Te Aroha March 6 over 1150m, soft track; 5 len behind Emma Twigg with 57kg at $15.50. Will be better for the run.

runners.name: LIT SHOW
runners.comment: First-starter by Super Seth out of Ipso Facto. Hard to recommend without a market push.

H:NFP:5:2026-07-09.comment: 2. MIND ON MY MONI lines up for the first time. By Ready For Moni and hails from the Vernon Beachy stable. Has the benefit of a good draw. Has the right credentials to play a major role. 3. ROSE RUN CHIEF is a two-year-old gelding by Volstead from Rose Run Value on debut. Has the benefit of a good draw. Has the right credentials to play a major role. 1. TOMISTRESS is a two-year-old filly from the Micah Miller stable on debut. Has the benefit of a good draw. Well-placed to score at her first appearance. 5. WALSTEAD is at the track for the first time. By Volstead and hails from the Jerry Miller stable. Will be right there at the business end.

runners.name: ROSE RUN CHIEF
runners.comment: Two-year-old gelding (Volstead - Rose Run Value) making his debut. Finished second at Northfield Park in a trial on June 14 over 1609m. Early signs are positive - looks the one to beat.

runners.name: TOMISTRESS
runners.comment: Two-year-old filly (Long Tom - Celebrity Faith) making her debut. Finished fourth in a trial on June 14 at Northfield Park over 1609m. Profiles nicely but betting will hold the key.

runners.name: LAVISH REIGN
runners.comment: Two-year-old filly by Global Republic S out of Lavish Beauty having her first start. Finished eighth in a trial on June 29 at Scioto Downs over 1609m. Worth keeping safe.

runners.name: MIND ON MY MONI
runners.comment: Two-year-old colt (Ready For Moni - Jj's Jordyn) making his debut. Showed ability when he won at The Meadows in a trial on June 23 over 1609m. Profiles nicely but betting will hold the key.

runners.name: WALSTEAD

runners.name: ANGEL SPIRIT
runners.comment: Two-year-old colt (Guardian Angel As - Spirit Bell) making his debut. Finished second in a trial on July 2 at Northfield Park over 1609m. Might be better for this outing

runners.name: CRESCENT
runners.comment: Two-year-old colt (Long Tom - I'm An Angel) making his debut. Finished third at The Meadows in a trial on June 23 over 1609m. Prefer to see at the races before recommending.

runners.name: KRAZY CAVIAR
runners.comment: Two-year-old gelding by Triumphant Caviar out of Crazy Smart on debut. Finished eighth at Scioto Downs in a trial on June 15 over 1609m. Betting will prove to be the best pointer

R:PNN:5:2026-07-09.comment: 1. TRY TO MAKE CENTS is racing well. Scored at Delaware Park (Dirt) two back then finished second, beaten 1.5 lengths, over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) at the latest appearance three-and-a-half weeks ago. Should camp just off the leaders. Leading contender. 3. SERGEANT CAPPS returns from a break of four-and-a-half months. Consistent performer last prep whose most recent appearance resulted in a seventh over 1609m at Aqueduct. Finds the right race to launch another bright campaign. 5. TUFF LUCK was racing well without winning, finishing in the money on three consecutive occasions, before finishing fifth last time out over 1710m at Laurel. Has the gate speed to take up a forward position. Capable of testing this field. 2. FENTON FOREVER is a promising galloper who resumes from a break. Last raced when third over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) a year ago which was the only run of that prep. Has never missed the frame when fresh from a spell in two starts. Has the pace to settle in the leading division. One of the better hopes.

runners.name: TRY TO MAKE CENTS
runners.comment: Form hard to fault. Last start 2nd in a small field at Parx on June 15 over 1609m, 1.5 len behind El Cordobes with 55.5kg at $11.60. The start before that won by 2.5 len at Delaware Park May 30 over 1710m defeating The Great Oscar with 55.5kg at $3.50. Has the ability to be in the finish.

runners.name: BRINDANO
runners.comment: At the latest run got back early 3rd in a small field at Laurel on June 20 over 1609m, 8 len behind Jolly By Golly carrying 56kg at $3.10. The start before that 2nd in a small field at Laurel on May 22 over 1710m, 5.5 len behind Preacha Meyers carrying 53.5kg at $10.20. Better off investing elsewhere.

runners.name: FENTON FOREVER
runners.comment: Back after a long spell. Only had one start last preparation tracked the speed and boxed on steadily 3rd of 7 at Parx on July 9 over 1609m, 7 len behind Light Forever with 54kg at $5.30. Won previous preparation first up scored a big 3.5 len win at Parx November 18 2024 over 1710m defeating Reelin N Dealin with 55.5kg at $3.50. Has the ability to run well.

runners.name: TUFF LUCK
runners.comment: Returns from a 38-day let-up. Most recently 5th of 8 at Laurel on May 31 over 1710m, 3.5 len behind Guaponess carrying 56kg at $3.60. The run before that 2nd in a small field at Laurel on May 14 over 1609m, 2.75 len behind El De Larry with 53.5kg at $2.50. Best form should see him measure right up.

runners.name: SERGEANT CAPPS
runners.comment: Resuming after four-and-a-half months in the paddock. Finished off last campaign seventh of 9 at Aqueduct March 1 over 1609m, 5 len behind Ambition carrying 56kg at $11.63. When resuming last campaign ran last of 11 at Ellis Park Turf July 4 2025 over 1609m, 16 len behind Lebowski with 53.5kg at $25.12. Likely to feature.

runners.name: KAZ BRIO
runners.comment: Racing well recently. Comes off a 52 day let-up. At the latest run won by 2 len at Monmouth Park May 17 over 1673m defeating Tifosi with 54kg at $14.10. The run before that 4th of 7 at Aqueduct on March 20 over 1408m, 6 len behind First Blessing with 55.5kg at $11.52. Last-start winner who can measure up again.

runners.comment: Two-year-old filly (Rockin Image - Rolling Delight) making her debut. Showed ability when she won at Scioto Downs in a trial on June 29 over 1609m. Market the best guide to her chances.

runners.name: WEHAVETOTALK
runners.comment: Two-year-old filly by Jk Endofanera out of Talkative having her first start. Finished third in a trial on June 13 at Hoosier Park over 1609m. Early signs are positive - looks the one to beat.

H:NFP:6:2026-07-09.comment: 2. NOBLIGHT ran eighth last start at this track over 1609m, beaten 21 metres. Has ability and should be right in the finish. 3. THE ONYX DRAGON disappointed last time when seventh over 1609m at this track. Can bounce back. 5. LIL PLUGS is the best of the rest after finishing fifth over 1609m at this track last time out.

Then a handy effort when well beaten into second behind Easily Amused at this track over 1609m in a NW2500L4 on June 29 at $79.70. Big chance with the aid of the ace draw.

runners.name: THE ONYX DRAGON

He was third in a trial some time ago, and if he doesn't need the run, then he can be a quick improver. Follow the market moves. 5. ALEZANDO was only beaten by a short-head here just over 3 weeks ago, and she won't have to improve by much to go one better. 2. REQUISITE has been thereabouts all prep, and he gets another attempt on this surface. In an even field, he is worth including in all top 4 combos.

Finds the right race to launch another bright campaign. 5. TUFF LUCK was racing well without winning, finishing in the money on three consecutive occasions, before finishing fifth last time out over 1710m at Laurel. Has the gate speed to take up a forward position. Capable of testing this field. 2. FENTON FOREVER is a promising galloper who resumes from a break. Last raced when third over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) a year ago which was the only run of that prep. Has never missed the frame when fresh from a spell in two starts. Has the pace to settle in the leading division. One of the better hopes.

R:CBG:1:2026-07-09.comment: 1. FORCE OF LAW has been trying to win a race for some time, and ran an encouraging third here just over 7 weeks ago when making his poly track debut. The gap between runs is the only concern, but with Michael McNab in the saddle, he will be hard to beat. 4. BIT OF A DAGG was only fair on debut at the end of January after some solid trial form. He was third in a trial some time ago, and if he doesn't need the run, then he can be a quick improver. Follow the market moves. 5. ALEZANDO was only beaten by a short-head here just over 3 weeks ago, and she won't have to improve by much to go one better. 2. REQUISITE has been thereabouts all prep, and he gets another attempt on this surface. In an even field, he is worth including in all top 4 combos.

Scored at Delaware Park (Dirt) two back then finished second, beaten 1.5 lengths, over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) at the latest appearance three-and-a-half weeks ago. Should camp just off the leaders. Leading contender. 3. SERGEANT CAPPS returns from a break of four-and-a-half months. Consistent performer last prep whose most recent appearance resulted in a seventh over 1609m at Aqueduct. Finds the right race to launch another bright campaign. 5. TUFF LUCK was racing well without winning, finishing in the money on three consecutive occasions, before finishing fifth last time out over 1710m at Laurel. Has the gate speed to take up a forward position. Capable of testing this field. 2. FENTON FOREVER is a promising galloper who resumes from a break. Last raced when third over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) a year ago which was the only run of that prep. Has never missed the frame when fresh from a spell in two starts. Has the pace to settle in the leading division. One of the better hopes.

R:CBG:1:2026-07-09.comment: 1. FORCE OF LAW has been trying to win a race for some time, and ran an encouraging third here just over 7 weeks ago when making his poly track debut. The gap between runs is the only concern, but with Michael McNab in the saddle, he will be hard to beat. 4. BIT OF A DAGG was only fair on debut at the end of January after some solid trial form. He was third in a trial some time ago, and if he doesn't need the run, then he can be a quick improver. Follow the market moves. 5. ALEZANDO was only beaten by a short-head here just over 3 weeks ago, and she won't have to improve by much to go one better. 2. REQUISITE has been thereabouts all prep, and he gets another attempt on this surface. In an even field, he is worth including in all top 4 combos.

R:CBG:1:2026-07-09.comment: 1. FORCE OF LAW has been trying to win a race for some time, and ran an encouraging third here just over 7 weeks ago when making his poly track debut. The gap between runs is the only concern, but with Michael McNab in the saddle, he will be hard to beat. 4. BIT OF A DAGG was only fair on debut at the end of January after some solid trial form. He was third in a trial some time ago, and if he doesn't need the run, then he can be a quick improver. Follow the market moves. 5. ALEZANDO was only beaten by a short-head here just over 3 weeks ago, and she won't have to improve by much to go one better. 2. REQUISITE has been thereabouts all prep, and he gets another attempt on this surface. In an even field, he is worth including in all top 4 combos.

runners.name: FORCE OF LAW
runners.comment: Comes here off a 50-day let-up. At the latest run came from midfield; 3rd of 11 at this track on May 20 over 1300m, 2.25 len behind Proud Capitalist with 55.5kg at $6.70. The run before that did the work up front then tired 5th of 14 at Woodville on April 29 over 1400m, slow going 1.2 len behind Timex with 58.5kg at $5.40. Looks one of the major contenders.

Before that raced behind the speed prior to fading 8th of 11 at Tauranga on May 29 over 1400m, 4 len behind Miss Silhouette with 58.5kg at $3.90. Strong chance to break though.

R:PNN:5:2026-07-09.comment: 1. TRY TO MAKE CENTS is racing well. Scored at Delaware Park (Dirt) two back then finished second, beaten 1.5 lengths, over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) at the latest appearance three-and-a-half weeks ago. Should camp just off the leaders. Leading contender. 3. SERGEANT CAPPS returns from a break of four-and-a-half months. Consistent performer last prep whose most recent appearance resulted in a seventh over 1609m at Aqueduct. Finds the right race to launch another bright campaign. 5. TUFF LUCK was racing well without winning, finishing in the money on three consecutive occasions, before finishing fifth last time out over 1710m at Laurel. Has the gate speed to take up a forward position. Capable of testing this field. 2. FENTON FOREVER is a promising galloper who resumes from a break. Last raced when third over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) a year ago which was the only run of that prep. Has never missed the frame when fresh from a spell in two starts. Has the pace to settle in the leading division. One of the better hopes.

R:CBG:1:2026-07-09.comment: 1. FORCE OF LAW has been trying to win a race for some time, and ran an encouraging third here just over 7 weeks ago when making his poly track debut. The gap between runs is the only concern, but with Michael McNab in the saddle, he will be hard to beat. 4. BIT OF A DAGG was only fair on debut at the end of January after some solid trial form. He was third in a trial some time ago, and if he doesn't need the run, then he can be a quick improver. Follow the market moves. 5. ALEZANDO was only beaten by a short-head here just over 3 weeks ago, and she won't have to improve by much to go one better. 2. REQUISITE has been thereabouts all prep, and he gets another attempt on this surface. In an even field, he is worth including in all top 4 combos.

R:PNN:5:2026-07-09.comment: 1. TRY TO MAKE CENTS is racing well. Scored at Delaware Park (Dirt) two back then finished second, beaten 1.5 lengths, over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) at the latest appearance three-and-a-half weeks ago. Should camp just off the leaders. Leading contender. 3. SERGEANT CAPPS returns from a break of four-and-a-half months. Consistent performer last prep whose most recent appearance resulted in a seventh over 1609m at Aqueduct. Finds the right race to launch another bright campaign. 5. TUFF LUCK was racing well without winning, finishing in the money on three consecutive occasions, before finishing fifth last time out over 1710m at Laurel. Has the gate speed to take up a forward position. Capable of testing this field. 2. FENTON FOREVER is a promising galloper who resumes from a break. Last raced when third over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) a year ago which was the only run of that prep. Has never missed the frame when fresh from a spell in two starts. Has the pace to settle in the leading division. One of the better hopes.

ref.race_name: BARRY & BETTY HARVEY 50 YEARS IN RACING

R:CBG:1:2026-07-09.comment: 1. FORCE OF LAW has been trying to win a race for some time, and ran an encouraging third here just over 7 weeks ago when making his poly track debut. The gap between runs is the only concern, but with Michael McNab in the saddle, he will be hard to beat. 4. BIT OF A DAGG was only fair on debut at the end of January after some solid trial form. He was third in a trial some time ago, and if he doesn't need the run, then he can be a quick improver. Follow the market moves. 5. ALEZANDO was only beaten by a short-head here just over 3 weeks ago, and she won't have to improve by much to go one better. 2. REQUISITE has been thereabouts all prep, and he gets another attempt on this surface. In an even field, he is worth including in all top 4 combos.

runners.name: FORCE OF LAW
runners.comment: Comes here off a 50-day let-up. At the latest run came from midfield; 3rd of 11 at this track on May 20 over 1300m, 2.25 len behind Proud Capitalist with 55.5kg at $6.70. The run before that did the work up front then tired 5th of 14 at Woodville on April 29 over 1400m, slow going 1.2 len behind Timex with 58.5kg at $5.40. Looks one of the major contenders.

runners.name: REQUISITE
runners.comment: Last start sat on the speed and boxed on steadily 4th of 8 at Arawa Park on July 1 over 1215m, on a wet track; 2.75 len behind Echannay carrying 56.5kg at $3.20. Before that raced behind the speed prior to fading 8th of 11 at Tauranga on May 29 over 1400m, 4 len behind Miss Silhouette with 58.5kg at $3.90. Strong chance to break though.

runners.name: ALEZANDO
runners.trainer: G A & D Rogerson & C Isdale
runners.comment: Third-up today. Resuming 5th of 10 at this track on May 20 over 970m, 4.5 len behind Angels Brew with 56.5kg at $7.30. Last start raced on speed throughout when 2nd of 11 at this track on June 17 over 1300m, head behind Buccino with 56.5kg at $11. Fitter now and making headway. Will take plenty of beating.

runners.name: THREEZ COMPANY
runners.comment: At debut, 7th of 11 at Pukekohe on June 24 over 1200m, in the wet; 10 len behind Elite Choice with 55.5kg at $6.50. No surprise to see her in the finish.

runners.name: IN THE VINEYARD
runners.comment: Second-up. First-up after three months 4th of 10 at this track on June 17 over 970m, 3.5 len behind Smashing Sister with 53.5kg at $4.70. Previously second-up 2nd in a small field at New Plymouth Raceway on January 24 over 1200m, on rain affected going; 2.75 len behind King Of The Air with 54.5kg at $2.60. Rates highly and should be in the mix.

runners.name: BIT OF A DAGG
runners.comment: Resuming after a lengthy spell. Only one run when sat behind the speed prior to fading 8th of 10 at Te Rapa on January 30 over 1300m, on a slow track; 7 len behind Mosala carrying 57.5kg at $7.40. Looks all set for a positive return.

runners.name: BY DEFAULT
runners.comment: Resuming from a lengthy break. Only one run when 7th of 11 at New Plymouth Raceway Mdn on November 5 over 1600m, 6 len behind Shamwari with 56.5kg at $18.30. Others look better.

runners.name: ANNIE BLING
runners.comment: Last start 5th of 11 at this track on June 17 over 1300m, 3 len behind Buccino carrying 56.5kg at $30.60. Promising signs last time and is worth including.

runners.name: LOMASI
runners.comment: At debut, 7th of 12 at Matamata on June 14 over 1400m, on a slow track; 5.5 len behind Dare To Proisir carrying 55.5kg at $11.80. Can do better. Plenty of scope for improvement.

runners.name: DARE YOU
runners.comment: Last start didn't do much in the run home; 8th of 10 at Te Aroha on June 21 over 1400m, on rain affected going; 11 len behind Sugar Daddy carrying 52.5kg at $15.80. runners.name: JUANITA
runners.comment: First starter by Vanbrugh out of Itsonlymakebelieve. Third May 26 at Te Awamutu at her only trial. Favouring others until she proves herself.

runners.name: MILLENNIUM MAGIC
runners.comment: She is resuming after a six-and-a-half months break. runners.name: TREASURE HUNT
runners.comment: She is resuming after a three-and-a-half months break. On debut ninth of 11 at Te Aroha March 6 over 1150m, soft track; 5 len behind Emma Twigg with 57kg at $15.50. Will be better for the run.

runners.name: LIT SHOW
runners.comment: First-starter by Super Seth out of Ipso Facto. Hard to recommend without a market push.

runners.comment: Two-year-old colt (Ready For Moni - Jj's Jordyn) making his debut. Showed ability when he won at The Meadows in a trial on June 23 over 1609m. Profiles nicely but betting will hold the key.

runners.comment: Two-year-old gelding by Triumphant Caviar out of Crazy Smart on debut. Finished eighth at Scioto Downs in a trial on June 15 over 1609m. Betting will prove to be the best pointer

R:PNN:5:2026-07-09.comment: 1. TRY TO MAKE CENTS is racing well. Scored at Delaware Park (Dirt) two back then finished second, beaten 1.5 lengths, over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) at the latest appearance three-and-a-half weeks ago. Should camp just off the leaders. Leading contender. 3. SERGEANT CAPPS returns from a break of four-and-a-half months. Consistent performer last prep whose most recent appearance resulted in a seventh over 1609m at Aqueduct. Finds the right race to launch another bright campaign. 5. TUFF LUCK was racing well without winning, finishing in the money on three consecutive occasions, before finishing fifth last time out over 1710m at Laurel. Has the gate speed to take up a forward position. Capable of testing this field. 2. FENTON FOREVER is a promising galloper who resumes from a break. Last raced when third over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) a year ago which was the only run of that prep. Has never missed the frame when fresh from a spell in two starts. Has the pace to settle in the leading division. One of the better hopes.

runners.name: TRY TO MAKE CENTS
runners.comment: Form hard to fault. Last start 2nd in a small field at Parx on June 15 over 1609m, 1.5 len behind El Cordobes with 55.5kg at $11.60. The start before that won by 2.5 len at Delaware Park May 30 over 1710m defeating The Great Oscar with 55.5kg at $3.50. Has the ability to be in the finish.

runners.name: BRINDANO
runners.comment: At the latest run got back early 3rd in a small field at Laurel on June 20 over 1609m, 8 len behind Jolly By Golly carrying 56kg at $3.10. The start before that 2nd in a small field at Laurel on May 22 over 1710m, 5.5 len behind Preacha Meyers carrying 53.5kg at $10.20. Better off investing elsewhere.

runners.name: FENTON FOREVER
runners.comment: Back after a long spell. Only had one start last preparation tracked the speed and boxed on steadily 3rd of 7 at Parx on July 9 over 1609m, 7 len behind Light Forever with 54kg at $5.30. Won previous preparation first up scored a big 3.5 len win at Parx November 18 2024 over 1710m defeating Reelin N Dealin with 55.5kg at $3.50. Has the ability to run well.

runners.name: TUFF LUCK
runners.comment: Returns from a 38-day let-up. Most recently 5th of 8 at Laurel on May 31 over 1710m, 3.5 len behind Guaponess carrying 56kg at $3.60. The run before that 2nd in a small field at Laurel on May 14 over 1609m, 2.75 len behind El De Larry with 53.5kg at $2.50. Best form should see him measure right up.

runners.name: SERGEANT CAPPS
runners.comment: Resuming after four-and-a-half months in the paddock. Finished off last campaign seventh of 9 at Aqueduct March 1 over 1609m, 5 len behind Ambition carrying 56kg at $11.63. When resuming last campaign ran last of 11 at Ellis Park Turf July 4 2025 over 1609m, 16 len behind Lebowski with 53.5kg at $25.12. Likely to feature.

runners.name: KAZ BRIO

The run before that 4th of 7 at Aqueduct on March 20 over 1408m, 6 len behind First Blessing with 55.5kg at $11.52. Last-start winner who can measure up again.

Should be ready to show her best now so she is a strong chance to break through. 8. JOHN D was narrowly beaten into second when second up last start at this track (Dirt) over 320m. Will drift back but wind up late. Will be doing his best work late.

runners.comment: Third-up today. Resuming 4th of 10 at this track on May 30 over 366m, 1.0 len behind Sydney Jack carrying 57kg at $9.10. Second run from a spell 4th of 10 at this track in LR on June 18 over 366m, 2 len behind Tw Streakin Cowboy with 57kg at $75.90. Should be near his top.

runners.name: FAV STREAKIN CARTE
runners.comment: Third-up today. Resumes off a 58 day letup. First-up got back early 5th of 10 at this track on April 8 over 274m, 2.5 len behind Send Me A Dynasty Hg with 57kg at $24.40. Second run from a spell 2nd of 10 at this track on May 11 over 320m, 0.1 len behind Run Dont Walk with 57kg at $14.20. Fitter now and making headway. Will take beating.

runners.comment: At the latest run raced on the pace when 3rd of 10 at this track on June 13 over 274m, 0.8 len behind Philomina with 57kg at $5.30. ref.race_name: BARRY & BETTY HARVEY 50 YEARS IN RACING

R:CBG:1:2026-07-09.comment: 1. FORCE OF LAW has been trying to win a race for some time, and ran an encouraging third here just over 7 weeks ago when making his poly track debut. The gap between runs is the only concern, but with Michael McNab in the saddle, he will be hard to beat. 4. BIT OF A DAGG was only fair on debut at the end of January after some solid trial form. He was third in a trial some time ago, and if he doesn't need the run, then he can be a quick improver. Follow the market moves. 5. ALEZANDO was only beaten by a short-head here just over 3 weeks ago, and she won't have to improve by much to go one better. 2. REQUISITE has been thereabouts all prep, and he gets another attempt on this surface. In an even field, he is worth including in all top 4 combos.

runners.name: FORCE OF LAW
runners.comment: Comes here off a 50-day let-up. At the latest run came from midfield; 3rd of 11 at this track on May 20 over 1300m, 2.25 len behind Proud Capitalist with 55.5kg at $6.70. The run before that did the work up front then tired 5th of 14 at Woodville on April 29 over 1400m, slow going 1.2 len behind Timex with 58.5kg at $5.40. Looks one of the major contenders.

runners.name: REQUISITE
runners.comment: Last start sat on the speed and boxed on steadily 4th of 8 at Arawa Park on July 1 over 1215m, on a wet track; 2.75 len behind Echannay carrying 56.5kg at $3.20. Before that raced behind the speed prior to fading 8th of 11 at Tauranga on May 29 over 1400m, 4 len behind Miss Silhouette with 58.5kg at $3.90. Strong chance to break though.

runners.name: ALEZANDO
runners.trainer: G A & D Rogerson & C Isdale
runners.comment: Third-up today. Resuming 5th of 10 at this track on May 20 over 970m, 4.5 len behind Angels Brew with 56.5kg at $7.30. Last start raced on speed throughout when 2nd of 11 at this track on June 17 over 1300m, head behind Buccino with 56.5kg at $11. Fitter now and making headway. Will take plenty of beating.

runners.name: THREEZ COMPANY
runners.comment: At debut, 7th of 11 at Pukekohe on June 24 over 1200m, in the wet; 10 len behind Elite Choice with 55.5kg at $6.50. No surprise to see her in the finish.

runners.name: IN THE VINEYARD
runners.comment: Second-up. First-up after three months 4th of 10 at this track on June 17 over 970m, 3.5 len behind Smashing Sister with 53.5kg at $4.70. Previously second-up 2nd in a small field at New Plymouth Raceway on January 24 over 1200m, on rain affected going; 2.75 len behind King Of The Air with 54.5kg at $2.60. Rates highly and should be in the mix.

runners.name: BIT OF A DAGG
runners.comment: Resuming after a lengthy spell. Only one run when sat behind the speed prior to fading 8th of 10 at Te Rapa on January 30 over 1300m, on a slow track; 7 len behind Mosala carrying 57.5kg at $7.40. Looks all set for a positive return.

runners.name: BY DEFAULT
runners.comment: Resuming from a lengthy break. Only one run when 7th of 11 at New Plymouth Raceway Mdn on November 5 over 1600m, 6 len behind Shamwari with 56.5kg at $18.30. Others look better.

runners.comment: At debut, 7th of 12 at Matamata on June 14 over 1400m, on a slow track; 5.5 len behind Dare To Proisir carrying 55.5kg at $11.80. Can do better. Plenty of scope for improvement.

runners.name: DARE YOU

runners.name: JUANITA
runners.comment: First starter by Vanbrugh out of Itsonlymakebelieve. Third May 26 at Te Awamutu at her only trial. Favouring others until she proves herself.

runners.name: MILLENNIUM MAGIC

runners.name: TREASURE HUNT
runners.comment: She is resuming after a three-and-a-half months break. runners.name: LIT SHOW
runners.comment: First-starter by Super Seth out of Ipso Facto. Hard to recommend without a market push.

runners.comment: Two-year-old gelding by Triumphant Caviar out of Crazy Smart on debut. Finished eighth at Scioto Downs in a trial on June 15 over 1609m. Betting will prove to be the best pointer

R:PNN:5:2026-07-09.comment: 1. TRY TO MAKE CENTS is racing well. Scored at Delaware Park (Dirt) two back then finished second, beaten 1.5 lengths, over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) at the latest appearance three-and-a-half weeks ago. Should camp just off the leaders. Leading contender. 3. SERGEANT CAPPS returns from a break of four-and-a-half months. Consistent performer last prep whose most recent appearance resulted in a seventh over 1609m at Aqueduct. Finds the right race to launch another bright campaign. 5. TUFF LUCK was racing well without winning, finishing in the money on three consecutive occasions, before finishing fifth last time out over 1710m at Laurel. Has the gate speed to take up a forward position. Capable of testing this field. 2. FENTON FOREVER is a promising galloper who resumes from a break. Last raced when third over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) a year ago which was the only run of that prep. Has never missed the frame when fresh from a spell in two starts. Has the pace to settle in the leading division. One of the better hopes.

runners.name: TRY TO MAKE CENTS
runners.comment: Form hard to fault. Last start 2nd in a small field at Parx on June 15 over 1609m, 1.5 len behind El Cordobes with 55.5kg at $11.60. The start before that won by 2.5 len at Delaware Park May 30 over 1710m defeating The Great Oscar with 55.5kg at $3.50. Has the ability to be in the finish.

runners.comment: At the latest run got back early 3rd in a small field at Laurel on June 20 over 1609m, 8 len behind Jolly By Golly carrying 56kg at $3.10. The start before that 2nd in a small field at Laurel on May 22 over 1710m, 5.5 len behind Preacha Meyers carrying 53.5kg at $10.20. Better off investing elsewhere.

runners.name: TUFF LUCK
runners.comment: Returns from a 38-day let-up. Most recently 5th of 8 at Laurel on May 31 over 1710m, 3.5 len behind Guaponess carrying 56kg at $3.60. The run before that 2nd in a small field at Laurel on May 14 over 1609m, 2.75 len behind El De Larry with 53.5kg at $2.50. Best form should see him measure right up.

runners.name: SERGEANT CAPPS
runners.comment: Resuming after four-and-a-half months in the paddock. Finished off last campaign seventh of 9 at Aqueduct March 1 over 1609m, 5 len behind Ambition carrying 56kg at $11.63. When resuming last campaign ran last of 11 at Ellis Park Turf July 4 2025 over 1609m, 16 len behind Lebowski with 53.5kg at $25.12. Likely to feature.

R:CBG:1:2026-07-09.comment: 1. FORCE OF LAW has been trying to win a race for some time, and ran an encouraging third here just over 7 weeks ago when making his poly track debut. The gap between runs is the only concern, but with Michael McNab in the saddle, he will be hard to beat. 4. BIT OF A DAGG was only fair on debut at the end of January after some solid trial form. He was third in a trial some time ago, and if he doesn't need the run, then he can be a quick improver. Follow the market moves. 5. ALEZANDO was only beaten by a short-head here just over 3 weeks ago, and she won't have to improve by much to go one better. 2. REQUISITE has been thereabouts all prep, and he gets another attempt on this surface. In an even field, he is worth including in all top 4 combos.

Finds the right race to launch another bright campaign. 5. TUFF LUCK was racing well without winning, finishing in the money on three consecutive occasions, before finishing fifth last time out over 1710m at Laurel. Last raced when third over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) a year ago which was the only run of that prep. Has never missed the frame when fresh from a spell in two starts. ALEZANDO was only beaten by a short-head here just over 3 weeks ago, and she won't have to improve by much to go one better. 2. R:PNN:5:2026-07-09.comment: 1. TRY TO MAKE CENTS is racing well. Scored at Delaware Park (Dirt) two back then finished second, beaten 1.5 lengths, over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) at the latest appearance three-and-a-half weeks ago. Should camp just off the leaders. Leading contender. 3. SERGEANT CAPPS returns from a break of four-and-a-half months. Consistent performer last prep whose most recent appearance resulted in a seventh over 1609m at Aqueduct. Finds the right race to launch another bright campaign. 5. TUFF LUCK was racing well without winning, finishing in the money on three consecutive occasions, before finishing fifth last time out over 1710m at Laurel. Has the gate speed to take up a forward position. Capable of testing this field. 2. FENTON FOREVER is a promising galloper who resumes from a break. Last raced when third over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) a year ago which was the only run of that prep. Has never missed the frame when fresh from a spell in two starts. Has the pace to settle in the leading division. One of the better hopes.

runners.name: TRY TO MAKE CENTS
runners.comment: Form hard to fault. Last start 2nd in a small field at Parx on June 15 over 1609m, 1.5 len behind El Cordobes with 55.5kg at $11.60. The start before that won by 2.5 len at Delaware Park May 30 over 1710m defeating The Great Oscar with 55.5kg at $3.50. Has the ability to be in the finish.

runners.name: BRINDANO
runners.comment: At the latest run got back early 3rd in a small field at Laurel on June 20 over 1609m, 8 len behind Jolly By Golly carrying 56kg at $3.10. The start before that 2nd in a small field at Laurel on May 22 over 1710m, 5.5 len behind Preacha Meyers carrying 53.5kg at $10.20. Better off investing elsewhere.

runners.name: FENTON FOREVER
runners.comment: Back after a long spell. Only had one start last preparation tracked the speed and boxed on steadily 3rd of 7 at Parx on July 9 over 1609m, 7 len behind Light Forever with 54kg at $5.30. Won previous preparation first up scored a big 3.5 len win at Parx November 18 2024 over 1710m defeating Reelin N Dealin with 55.5kg at $3.50. Has the ability to run well.

runners.name: TUFF LUCK
runners.comment: Returns from a 38-day let-up. Most recently 5th of 8 at Laurel on May 31 over 1710m, 3.5 len behind Guaponess carrying 56kg at $3.60. The run before that 2nd in a small field at Laurel on May 14 over 1609m, 2.75 len behind El De Larry with 53.5kg at $2.50. Best form should see him measure right up.

runners.name: SERGEANT CAPPS
runners.comment: Resuming after four-and-a-half months in the paddock. Finished off last campaign seventh of 9 at Aqueduct March 1 over 1609m, 5 len behind Ambition carrying 56kg at $11.63. When resuming last campaign ran last of 11 at Ellis Park Turf July 4 2025 over 1609m, 16 len behind Lebowski with 53.5kg at $25.12. Likely to feature.

runners.name: KAZ BRIO
runners.comment: Racing well recently. Comes off a 52 day let-up. At the latest run won by 2 len at Monmouth Park May 17 over 1673m defeating Tifosi with 54kg at $14.10. The run before that 4th of 7 at Aqueduct on March 20 over 1408m, 6 len behind First Blessing with 55.5kg at $11.52. Last-start winner who can measure up again.

ref.race_name: INDIANA SIRE STAKES ELIGIBLES PACE MS

H:HOO:6:2026-07-09.comment: 4. WEHAVETOTALK faces the starter for the first time. By Jk Endofanera and hails from the Erv Miller stable. Will be ready to run a big race. 3. SWEET IMAGE is a two-year-old filly from the Roger Welch yard on debut. Has the benefit of a good draw. Sure to prove very hard to beat. 5. SHADY LADY LANA is better than her last-start eighth over 1609m at this track. 1. PETE'S FREAKY DATE is a two-year-old filly by Freaky Feet Pete from Pacific Date on debut. Will enjoy a nice run from this favourable starting position. Any push in betting will add to the confidence.

runners.name: PETE'S FREAKY DATE
runners.comment: Two-year-old filly by Freaky Feet Pete out of Pacific Date on debut. Finished fourth in a trial on July 1 at Hoosier Park over 1609m. Respect any move in betting.

runners.name: SWEET IMAGE
runners.comment: Two-year-old filly (Rockin Image - Sweet Yankee Lou) making her debut. Finished sixth at Hoosier Park in a trial on June 27 over 1609m. Early signs are positive - looks the one to beat.

runners.name: ROCKING RHONDA
runners.comment: Two-year-old filly (Rockin Image - Rolling Delight) making her debut. Showed ability when she won at Scioto Downs in a trial on June 29 over 1609m. Market the best guide to her chances.

runners.name: WEHAVETOTALK
runners.comment: Two-year-old filly by Jk Endofanera out of Talkative having her first start. Finished third in a trial on June 13 at Hoosier Park over 1609m. Early signs are positive - looks the one to beat.

runners.name: SHADY LADY LANA
runners.comment: Last start 8th (Fr7) over 1609m at this track, beaten 65 m by Callie Rocks (1:53.30) in IS2FNW1PM on June 30 at $32.40. Before that, pulled up at this track on June 16 over 1609m. Hard to leave out.

H:NFP:6:2026-07-09.comment: 2. NOBLIGHT ran eighth last start at this track over 1609m, beaten 21 metres. Has ability and should be right in the finish. 3. THE ONYX DRAGON disappointed last time when seventh over 1609m at this track. Can bounce back. 5. LIL PLUGS is the best of the rest after finishing fifth over 1609m at this track last time out.

runners.name: DREAMINALLTHETIME
runners.comment: Two back was well beaten into sixth behind Thornbush Hanover at this track over 1609m in a NW2500L4 on June 22 at $69.40. Then a handy effort when well beaten into second behind Easily Amused at this track over 1609m in a NW2500L4 on June 29 at $79.70. Big chance with the aid of the ace draw.

runners.name: THE ONYX DRAGON
runners.comment: Recent efforts haven't been up to his best. Last start 7th (Fr3) over 1609m at this track beaten 20 m by Cocosdream (1:53.10) in NW3500L4 on June 28 at $101. The run before 5th (Fr4) over 1609m at this track, beaten 10 m by Smooth Baht (1:52.20) in NW5000L4 on June 21 at $65.10. Suitably placed and can bounce back.

runners.name: CAPTAIN ALBERT G
runners.comment: Well-beaten 10 days ago at this track over 1609m, and two back 5th (Fr2) over 1609m at this track beaten 12 m by Born To Run As (1:53.40) in NW2500L4 on June 1 at $39.80. Recent form hasn't been good enough.

runners.name: NOBLIGHT
runners.comment: Capable of better than we have seen lately. Last start 8th (Fr5) over 1609m at this track beaten 21 m by Master Yossi (1:52.00) in NW5000L4 on June 28 at $84.20. The start before 8th (Fr8) over 1609m at this track beaten 18 m by Smooth Baht (1:52.20) in NW5000L4 on June 21 at $116.10. Anticipating a much better performance this time.

runners.name: ROSE RUN XILED
runners.comment: Last start 9th (Fr2) over 1609m at this track, beaten 40 m by Sriracha Heat (1:54.10) in NW3500L4 on June 17 at $28.50. At the start before 7th (Fr7) over 1609m at Scioto Downs, beaten 70 m by Southbeach Hanover (1:52.30) in HGNW3751L4 on June 3 at $56.80. Has the ability to take this out.

runners.name: LIL PLUGS
runners.comment: Last start 5th (Fr6) over 1609m at this track beaten 21 m by Our Benson (1:54.00) in NW2500L4 on June 29 at $62.80. Before that, 7th (Fr1) over 1609m at this track beaten 12 m by Born Wild (1:53.40) in NW5000L4 on June 15 at $19.30. Solid top-three prospects.

runners.name: UNION STATION
runners.comment: 10-year-old gelding showed improvement last time. Posted a big 8 m win (Fr1) at this track (1:56.10) beating Draco's Storm in NW1500L4 on June 28 at $4.50. The race before was not as impressive when well beaten into sixth behind Barley Mischievous at this track over 1609m in a NW1500L4 on June 17 at $33.10. Form reads okay, but out of his depth.

runners.name: KATE-N-AIDAN
runners.comment: 4th (Sr1) over 1609m at this track beaten 7 m by Heart Of Rock (1:56.20) in NW2500L4 on June 24 at $9.30. Before that 4th (Fr3) over 1609m at this track, beaten 35 m by Commander Bob (1:52.30) in NW2500L4 on June 10 at $84.90. Needs to improve.

runners.name: VOODOO RANGER
runners.comment: Last start 5th (Fr6) over 1609m at this track beaten 12 m by Tyler Albert (1:55.00) in 6000CL on June 29 at $99.80. Two runs back 7th (Fr2) over 1609m at this track, beaten 16 m by Feel My Wrath (1:56.10) in 6000CL on June 17 at $5.80. Others preferred at this stage.

R:HSE:11:2026-07-09.comment: 3. THREE EYED CANDY went back early from his wide draw over 366m in stakes class at this track (Dirt) last time but hit the line strongly to finish fourth. Look for another barnstorming finish. 5. FAV STREAKIN CARTEL was beaten just 0.1 lengths last start and landed his first top-three finish in the process at this track (Dirt) over 320m. Looks close to a win. 4. MADAM MIM was prominent from the outset last time and held on well for third over 274m at this track (Dirt). Should be ready to show her best now so she is a strong chance to break through. 8. JOHN D was narrowly beaten into second when second up last start at this track (Dirt) over 320m. Will drift back but wind up late. Will be doing his best work late.

runners.name: THREE EYED CANDY
runners.comment: Third-up today. Resuming 4th of 10 at this track on May 30 over 366m, 1.0 len behind Sydney Jack carrying 57kg at $9.10. Second run from a spell 4th of 10 at this track in LR on June 18 over 366m, 2 len behind Tw Streakin Cowboy with 57kg at $75.90. Should be near his top.

runners.name: FAV STREAKIN CARTE
runners.comment: Third-up today. Resumes off a 58 day letup. First-up got back early 5th of 10 at this track on April 8 over 274m, 2.5 len behind Send Me A Dynasty Hg with 57kg at $24.40. Second run from a spell 2nd of 10 at this track on May 11 over 320m, 0.1 len behind Run Dont Walk with 57kg at $14.20. Fitter now and making headway. Will take beating.

runners.name: MADAM MIM
runners.comment: At the latest run raced on the pace when 3rd of 10 at this track on June 13 over 274m, 0.8 len behind Philomina with 57kg at $5.30. The race before that tracked the speed and boxed on steadily 5th of 9 at this track on May 11 over 320m, 1.75 len behind Jess Sinful carrying 57kg at $10. Another bold run is expected.

runners.name: JOHN D
runners.comment: Third-up today. Resuming eased back early on 8th of 10 at this track on May 30 over 366m, 5 len behind Roses After Midnight carrying 57kg at $9.70. Last start 2nd of 10 at this track on June 17 over 320m, 0.5 len behind Flying Knockout 123 with 58kg at $2.20. Swooper to watch for late in the piece.

runners.name: LIL KOOL KATE
runners.comment: At the latest run 4th of 10 at this track on June 23 over 274m, 0.8 len behind Flyin Ivory carrying 57kg at $23.90. The run before that 6th of 8 at this track on May 21 over 320m, 3 len behind Ap Im That Dove carrying 59kg at $30.40. No surprise if she was in the finish.

runners.name: WANNA SWEET TREET
runners.comment: Comes here off a 39-day let-up. Most recently held ground to the line 3rd of 10 at this track on May 30 over 302m, 1.3 len behind Valiant Beacher carrying 58kg at $14.40. The run before that eased back early on 7th of 10 at this track on May 11 over 320m, 4 len behind Mi Blood Token carrying 58kg at $21.20. Not far away last time but hard to have in this contest.

runners.name: RHINO SIX
runners.comment: Comes here off a 34-day let-up. Most recently 2nd of 10 at this track on June 4 over 302m, 1.3 len behind Favorite First Prize with 59.5kg at $27. The run before that 7th of 10 at this track on May 11 over 320m, 3.5 len behind Perrys Secret Gold with 57kg at $84.80. Not far from the winner last time but this is a tough assignment.

runners.name: EASY LOVIN BEACH
runners.comment: Resumes from a spell. Finished off last preparation fifth of 10 at this track November 13 over 320m, 2.25 len behind Gv Little Guy with 56kg at $16.50. On debut ran last of 8 at this track June 23 2025 over 274m, 9 len behind Caelus carrying 56kg at $7.60. Others have firmer winning claims.

runners.name: S C SOTOL
runners.comment: Third-up today. First-up got back early 6th of 10 at this track on April 29 over 274m, 2 len behind Kiss Of Corona carrying 57kg at $18.20. Second run from a spell eased back early on 8th of 10 at this track on June 17 over 320m, 3.5 len behind Flying Knockout 123 carrying 58.5kg at $28.60. Prefer to watch at this stage.

runners.name: CORONAPOLLO
runners.comment: Third-up today. Resuming 8th of 10 at this track on April 15 over 274m, 4 len behind Keep Her Off Tequila carrying 57kg at $114. Second run from a spell settled back and ran on when 7th of 10 at this track on June 4 over 302m, 3.5 len behind Favorite First Prize carrying 57kg at $40.30. Likely to struggle.

H:NFP:4:2026-07-09.comment: 4. GREAT SERVICE finished fourth last start, beaten 12 metres at The Meadows over 1609m. Has ability and should be right in the finish. 5. SO SO DE VIE is in the mix following a fourth at his most recent appearance over 1609m at this track. 1. NORTHERN ANGLE is worth some thought after his last-start fifth over 1609m at this track on June 24.

runners.name: SO SO DE VIE
runners.comment: Last start 4th (Fr3) over 1609m at this track beaten 24 m by Moved Bythe Spirit (1:52.20) in NW2500L4 on June 24 at $9.50. Before that, 6th (Fr4) over 1609m at this track, beaten 30 m by Betterdealmein (1:52.40) in NW2500L4 on June 9 at $73.70. Genuine contender in this suitable contest.

runners.name: GREAT SERVICE
runners.comment: Last start 4th (Fr4) over 1609m at The Meadows beaten 12 m by Miki's Big Guy (1:52.20) in 7500CLHC on June 19 at $51.30. Before that, unplaced (Fr8) over 1609m at The Meadows well beaten by Miki's Big Guy (1:52.40) in 7500CLHC on June 13 at $50.60. Genuine contender in this suitable contest.

runners.name: DRAGONS R REAL Q
runners.comment: 4th (Fr1) over 1609m at this track, beaten 5 m by Nassau Desire (1:55.30) in 6000CL on June 24 at $9.70. Before that 6th (Fr4) over 1609m at this track beaten 22 m by Cruising Along (1:54.00) in NW4PMCD on June 8 at $44.30. Needs to improve.

runners.name: Y S MATHIS BRO
runners.comment: Last start 5th (Fr3) over 1609m at this track, beaten 23 m by Union Station (1:56.10) in NW1500L4 on June 28 at $6.10. Two runs back 3rd (Fr6) over 1609m at this track, beaten 4 m by Three Grand (1:55.40) in NW1500L4 on June 10 at $39.20. Hard to envisage a win.

runners.name: PRINCE JOHN
runners.comment: Last start 5th (Fr3) over 1609m at this track, beaten 55 m by Beach Master Keene (1:53.10) in NW1500L4 on June 28 at $4.10. Two runs back 3rd (Fr5) over 1609m at this track beaten 2.25 m by Odds On Pick Six (1:56.30) in NW1500L4 on June 22 at $58. Needs to find plenty of improvement to figure.

runners.name: NORTHERN ANGLE
runners.comment: Last start 5th (Fr7) over 1609m at this track, beaten 12 m by Nassau Desire (1:55.30) in 6000CL on June 24 at $85.60. Before that, a game effort when 3rd (Fr3) over 1609m at this track, beaten 10 m by Feel My Wrath (1:56.10) in 6000CL on June 17 at $73.60. About the mark of late so worth including.

runners.name: PETIPOU
runners.comment: Last start 5th (Fr5) over 1609m at this track, beaten 13 m by Over Hill N Delle (1:57.30) in NW1500L4 on May 26 at $8.50. Two runs back 3rd (Fr3) over 1609m at Buffalo Raceway, beaten 4 m by Mighty Santana (1:57.40) in NW1000L4 on May 8 at $12.28. Better off investing elsewhere.

runners.name: MAGIC SHARK
runners.comment: Didn't do much at the last run, 8th (Fr1) over 1609m at this track, beaten 22 m by Southwind Judge (1:55.10) in NW1500L4 on June 24 at $9.10. Before that 5th (Fr2) over 1609m at this track beaten 25 m by Voodoo Ranger (1:55.20) in NW1500L4 on June 9 at $44.80. Favour others.

runners.name: DOUG'S JAXX
runners.comment: Didn't do much at the last run, 8th (Fr6) over 1609m at this track beaten 25 m by Union Station (1:56.10) in NW1500L4 on June 28 at $5.80. Before that 6th (Fr4) over 1609m at this track, beaten 24 m by U Going (1:54.20) in NW2500L4 on June 14 at $62.20. Might find this too tough.

H:HOO:5:2026-07-09.comment: 5. JUST A START finished eighth last start over 1609m at this track, beaten 11 metres, after filling a minor placing at the previous appearance over 1609m at this track. Good enough to take out a race like this. 7. THOSESUMMERNIGHTS finished seventh at Northfield Park two runs ago before finishing seventh at her latest appearance over 1609m at Northfield Park, beaten 18 metres. Interesting to see what unfolds from this wide draw. Finds a suitable assignment and can bounce back to form. 6. MYSTICAL CHARGER didn't do a bad job when sixth over 1609m at this track last time out. 2. FOX VALLEY BOXER has drawn to advantage and should give backers a great sight.

runners.name: FOX VALLEY BOXER
runners.comment: Last start 4th (Fr8) over 1609m at this track beaten 15 m by Sure Thing Herman (1:52.20) in TMR?75 on July 1 at $48.70. Before that, 4th (Fr5) over 1006m at this track beaten 3 m by Salty Robyn (1:08.10) in 5000?CL on June 25 at $6.80. Not out of the equation.

runners.name: MISS SIXTEEN K
runners.comment: Resumes. Her previous first-up run was successful when she won by a 0.9 m (Fr6) at this track (1:53.00) beating P L Leeroy in TMR?70 on June 5 2025 at $3.90. Prefer her later in the campaign.

runners.name: JUST A START
runners.comment: Last start 8th (Fr4) over 1609m at this track, beaten 11 m by Cool Blues Man (1:53.10) in TMR?75 on June 27 at $4. Before that, a game effort when 3rd (Fr5) over 1609m at this track beaten 4 m by Fact Check (1:53.20) in TMR?75 on June 20 at $4.60. Big run expected.

runners.name: ANOTHER LEGEND
runners.comment: Last start 8th (Fr7) over 1609m at this track beaten 25 m by Fact Check (1:53.20) in TMR?75 on June 20 at $40.50. At the run before 4th (Fr4) over 1609m at this track, beaten 40 m by Somemore Soaring (1:50.40) in HGWO1-4PM on June 12 at $39.20. Must be included.

runners.name: MYSTICAL CHARGER
runners.comment: Recent form below what he is capable of. Last start 6th (Fr3) over 1609m at this track beaten 25 m by Sure Thing Herman (1:52.20) in TMR?75 on July 1 at $13.10. The start before 5th (Fr3) over 1609m at Oak Grove Racing beaten 6 m by Lil Joe Iv (1:53.20) in NW?4000?L4 on June 16 at $9.25. Suitably placed and can make amends.

runners.name: IMAREALROKNBUGATTI
runners.comment: Last start 4th (Fr5) over 1609m at this track beaten 7 m by Youremyobsession (1:54.00) in TMR?70 on June 23 at $3.20. Two runs back 5th (Fr2) over 1609m at this track, beaten 18 m by Knights Templar (1:53.30) in TMR?70 on June 16 at $3.40. Impossible to tip with any confidence.

runners.name: APERTIF
runners.comment: Last start 6th (Fr1) over 1609m at this track, beaten 6 m by Cool Blues Man (1:53.10) in TMR?75 on June 27 at $7.40. At his previous start solid effort when 3rd (Fr4) over 1609m at this track beaten 6 m by Seaside Hanover (1:52.30) in TMR?75 on June 20 at $4.90. Top-three finish is well within his grasp.

runners.name: THOSESUMMERNIGHTS
runners.comment: Recent form below what she is capable of. Last start 7th (Fr8) over 1609m at Northfield Park beaten 18 m by Jamie Amanda (1:55.40) in FMNW4PMCD on June 14 at $121.10. The run previous 7th (Fr6) over 1609m at Northfield Park, beaten 20 m by Itwasaverygoodyear (1:56.10) in 30000CLNW7 on May 26 at $40.90. Suitably placed and can turn things around.

runners.name: BIG LAW
runners.comment: Didn't do much last time out, 7th (Fr7) over 1609m at this track, beaten 35 m by Sure Thing Herman (1:52.20) in TMR?75 on July 1 at $40.20. Before that 4th (Fr2) over 1609m at this track, beaten 6 m by Seaside Hanover (1:52.30) in TMR?75 on June 20 at $11.30. Needs an easier race.

ref.race_name: BARRY & BETTY HARVEY 50 YEARS IN RACING

R:CBG:1:2026-07-09.comment: 1. FORCE OF LAW has been trying to win a race for some time, and ran an encouraging third here just over 7 weeks ago when making his poly track debut. The gap between runs is the only concern, but with Michael McNab in the saddle, he will be hard to beat. 4. BIT OF A DAGG was only fair on debut at the end of January after some solid trial form. He was third in a trial some time ago, and if he doesn't need the run, then he can be a quick improver. Follow the market moves. 5. ALEZANDO was only beaten by a short-head here just over 3 weeks ago, and she won't have to improve by much to go one better. 2. REQUISITE has been thereabouts all prep, and he gets another attempt on this surface. In an even field, he is worth including in all top 4 combos.

runners.name: FORCE OF LAW
runners.comment: Comes here off a 50-day let-up. At the latest run came from midfield; 3rd of 11 at this track on May 20 over 1300m, 2.25 len behind Proud Capitalist with 55.5kg at $6.70. The run before that did the work up front then tired 5th of 14 at Woodville on April 29 over 1400m, slow going 1.2 len behind Timex with 58.5kg at $5.40. Looks one of the major contenders.

runners.name: REQUISITE
runners.comment: Last start sat on the speed and boxed on steadily 4th of 8 at Arawa Park on July 1 over 1215m, on a wet track; 2.75 len behind Echannay carrying 56.5kg at $3.20. Before that raced behind the speed prior to fading 8th of 11 at Tauranga on May 29 over 1400m, 4 len behind Miss Silhouette with 58.5kg at $3.90. Strong chance to break though.

runners.name: ALEZANDO
runners.trainer: G A & D Rogerson & C Isdale
runners.comment: Third-up today. Resuming 5th of 10 at this track on May 20 over 970m, 4.5 len behind Angels Brew with 56.5kg at $7.30. Last start raced on speed throughout when 2nd of 11 at this track on June 17 over 1300m, head behind Buccino with 56.5kg at $11. Fitter now and making headway. Will take plenty of beating.

runners.name: THREEZ COMPANY
runners.comment: At debut, 7th of 11 at Pukekohe on June 24 over 1200m, in the wet; 10 len behind Elite Choice with 55.5kg at $6.50. No surprise to see her in the finish.

runners.name: IN THE VINEYARD
runners.comment: Second-up. First-up after three months 4th of 10 at this track on June 17 over 970m, 3.5 len behind Smashing Sister with 53.5kg at $4.70. Previously second-up 2nd in a small field at New Plymouth Raceway on January 24 over 1200m, on rain affected going; 2.75 len behind King Of The Air with 54.5kg at $2.60. Rates highly and should be in the mix.

runners.name: BIT OF A DAGG
runners.comment: Resuming after a lengthy spell. Only one run when sat behind the speed prior to fading 8th of 10 at Te Rapa on January 30 over 1300m, on a slow track; 7 len behind Mosala carrying 57.5kg at $7.40. Looks all set for a positive return.

runners.name: BY DEFAULT
runners.comment: Resuming from a lengthy break. Only one run when 7th of 11 at New Plymouth Raceway Mdn on November 5 over 1600m, 6 len behind Shamwari with 56.5kg at $18.30. Others look better.

runners.name: ANNIE BLING
runners.comment: Last start 5th of 11 at this track on June 17 over 1300m, 3 len behind Buccino carrying 56.5kg at $30.60. The run before that raced on the pace before fading 7th of 12 at Te Aroha on June 1 over 1400m, on a slow track; 6 len behind Brewster with 55.5kg at $16.90. Promising signs last time and is worth including.

runners.name: LOMASI
runners.comment: At debut, 7th of 12 at Matamata on June 14 over 1400m, on a slow track; 5.5 len behind Dare To Proisir carrying 55.5kg at $11.80. Can do better. Plenty of scope for improvement.

runners.name: DARE YOU
runners.comment: Last start didn't do much in the run home; 8th of 10 at Te Aroha on June 21 over 1400m, on rain affected going; 11 len behind Sugar Daddy carrying 52.5kg at $15.80. Before that came from midfield; 2nd of 10 at Tauranga on May 29 over 1400m, 3 len behind Misissipi Gambler with 52.5kg at $35.30. Can improve on recent efforts.

runners.name: JUANITA
runners.comment: First starter by Vanbrugh out of Itsonlymakebelieve. Third May 26 at Te Awamutu at her only trial. Favouring others until she proves herself.

runners.name: MILLENNIUM MAGIC
runners.comment: She is resuming after a six-and-a-half months break. Finished off last campaign ninth of 13 at Arawa Park December 27 over 1400m, soft track; 8 len behind Timbuctoo with 54kg at $36.70. On debut ninth of 14 at Te Aroha December 7 over 1400m, 5 len behind Unwritten with 56.5kg at $44.40. Can't enthuse.

runners.name: TRIPLE THREAT
runners.comment: Third-up today. First-up 2nd of 8 at Hawera on May 17 over 1200m, on a slow track; 0.3 len behind Tongue In Cheek with 58.5kg at $5.40. Last start ran last of 9 at Te Aroha on June 21 over 1150m, in the wet; 11 len behind Winner Patch carrying 58.5kg at $3.50. Better than the last effort suggests and deserves another chance.

runners.name: TREASURE HUNT
runners.comment: She is resuming after a three-and-a-half months break. Not seen since she finished last of 12 at Matamata F&M Mdn-Sw March 22 over 1400m, soft track; 11 len behind Artamos with 55kg at $34.90. On debut ninth of 11 at Te Aroha March 6 over 1150m, soft track; 5 len behind Emma Twigg with 57kg at $15.50. Will be better for the run.

R:PNN:5:2026-07-09.comment: 1. TRY TO MAKE CENTS is racing well. Scored at Delaware Park (Dirt) two back then finished second, beaten 1.5 lengths, over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) at the latest appearance three-and-a-half weeks ago. Should camp just off the leaders. Leading contender. 3. SERGEANT CAPPS returns from a break of four-and-a-half months. Consistent performer last prep whose most recent appearance resulted in a seventh over 1609m at Aqueduct. Finds the right race to launch another bright campaign. 5. TUFF LUCK was racing well without winning, finishing in the money on three consecutive occasions, before finishing fifth last time out over 1710m at Laurel. Has the gate speed to take up a forward position. Capable of testing this field. 2. FENTON FOREVER is a promising galloper who resumes from a break. Last raced when third over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) a year ago which was the only run of that prep. Has never missed the frame when fresh from a spell in two starts. Has the pace to settle in the leading division. One of the better hopes.

R:CBG:1:2026-07-09.comment: 1. FORCE OF LAW has been trying to win a race for some time, and ran an encouraging third here just over 7 weeks ago when making his poly track debut. The gap between runs is the only concern, but with Michael McNab in the saddle, he will be hard to beat. 4. BIT OF A DAGG was only fair on debut at the end of January after some solid trial form. He was third in a trial some time ago, and if he doesn't need the run, then he can be a quick improver. Follow the market moves. 5. ALEZANDO was only beaten by a short-head here just over 3 weeks ago, and she won't have to improve by much to go one better. 2. REQUISITE has been thereabouts all prep, and he gets another attempt on this surface. In an even field, he is worth including in all top 4 combos.

R:CBG:1:2026-07-09.comment: 1. FORCE OF LAW has been trying to win a race for some time, and ran an encouraging third here just over 7 weeks ago when making his poly track debut. The gap between runs is the only concern, but with Michael McNab in the saddle, he will be hard to beat. 4. BIT OF A DAGG was only fair on debut at the end of January after some solid trial form. He was third in a trial some time ago, and if he doesn't need the run, then he can be a quick improver. Follow the market moves. 5. ALEZANDO was only beaten by a short-head here just over 3 weeks ago, and she won't have to improve by much to go one better. 2. REQUISITE has been thereabouts all prep, and he gets another attempt on this surface. In an even field, he is worth including in all top 4 combos.

runners.name: FORCE OF LAW
runners.comment: Comes here off a 50-day let-up. At the latest run came from midfield; 3rd of 11 at this track on May 20 over 1300m, 2.25 len behind Proud Capitalist with 55.5kg at $6.70. The run before that did the work up front then tired 5th of 14 at Woodville on April 29 over 1400m, slow going 1.2 len behind Timex with 58.5kg at $5.40. Looks one of the major contenders.

R:PNN:5:2026-07-09.comment: 1. TRY TO MAKE CENTS is racing well. Scored at Delaware Park (Dirt) two back then finished second, beaten 1.5 lengths, over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) at the latest appearance three-and-a-half weeks ago. Should camp just off the leaders. Leading contender. 3. SERGEANT CAPPS returns from a break of four-and-a-half months. Consistent performer last prep whose most recent appearance resulted in a seventh over 1609m at Aqueduct. Finds the right race to launch another bright campaign. 5. TUFF LUCK was racing well without winning, finishing in the money on three consecutive occasions, before finishing fifth last time out over 1710m at Laurel. Has the gate speed to take up a forward position. Capable of testing this field. 2. FENTON FOREVER is a promising galloper who resumes from a break. Last raced when third over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) a year ago which was the only run of that prep. Has never missed the frame when fresh from a spell in two starts. Has the pace to settle in the leading division. One of the better hopes.

R:CBG:1:2026-07-09.comment: 1. FORCE OF LAW has been trying to win a race for some time, and ran an encouraging third here just over 7 weeks ago when making his poly track debut. The gap between runs is the only concern, but with Michael McNab in the saddle, he will be hard to beat. 4. BIT OF A DAGG was only fair on debut at the end of January after some solid trial form. He was third in a trial some time ago, and if he doesn't need the run, then he can be a quick improver. Follow the market moves. 5. ALEZANDO was only beaten by a short-head here just over 3 weeks ago, and she won't have to improve by much to go one better. 2. REQUISITE has been thereabouts all prep, and he gets another attempt on this surface. In an even field, he is worth including in all top 4 combos.

R:PNN:5:2026-07-09.comment: 1. TRY TO MAKE CENTS is racing well. Scored at Delaware Park (Dirt) two back then finished second, beaten 1.5 lengths, over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) at the latest appearance three-and-a-half weeks ago. Should camp just off the leaders. Leading contender. 3. SERGEANT CAPPS returns from a break of four-and-a-half months. Consistent performer last prep whose most recent appearance resulted in a seventh over 1609m at Aqueduct. Finds the right race to launch another bright campaign. 5. TUFF LUCK was racing well without winning, finishing in the money on three consecutive occasions, before finishing fifth last time out over 1710m at Laurel. Has the gate speed to take up a forward position. Capable of testing this field. 2. FENTON FOREVER is a promising galloper who resumes from a break. Last raced when third over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) a year ago which was the only run of that prep. Has never missed the frame when fresh from a spell in two starts. Has the pace to settle in the leading division. One of the better hopes.

ref.race_name: BARRY & BETTY HARVEY 50 YEARS IN RACING

R:CBG:1:2026-07-09.comment: 1. FORCE OF LAW has been trying to win a race for some time, and ran an encouraging third here just over 7 weeks ago when making his poly track debut. The gap between runs is the only concern, but with Michael McNab in the saddle, he will be hard to beat. 4. BIT OF A DAGG was only fair on debut at the end of January after some solid trial form. He was third in a trial some time ago, and if he doesn't need the run, then he can be a quick improver. Follow the market moves. 5. ALEZANDO was only beaten by a short-head here just over 3 weeks ago, and she won't have to improve by much to go one better. 2. REQUISITE has been thereabouts all prep, and he gets another attempt on this surface. In an even field, he is worth including in all top 4 combos.

runners.name: FORCE OF LAW
runners.comment: Comes here off a 50-day let-up. At the latest run came from midfield; 3rd of 11 at this track on May 20 over 1300m, 2.25 len behind Proud Capitalist with 55.5kg at $6.70. The run before that did the work up front then tired 5th of 14 at Woodville on April 29 over 1400m, slow going 1.2 len behind Timex with 58.5kg at $5.40. Looks one of the major contenders.

runners.name: REQUISITE
runners.comment: Last start sat on the speed and boxed on steadily 4th of 8 at Arawa Park on July 1 over 1215m, on a wet track; 2.75 len behind Echannay carrying 56.5kg at $3.20. Before that raced behind the speed prior to fading 8th of 11 at Tauranga on May 29 over 1400m, 4 len behind Miss Silhouette with 58.5kg at $3.90. Strong chance to break though.

runners.name: ALEZANDO
runners.trainer: G A & D Rogerson & C Isdale
runners.comment: Third-up today. Resuming 5th of 10 at this track on May 20 over 970m, 4.5 len behind Angels Brew with 56.5kg at $7.30. Last start raced on speed throughout when 2nd of 11 at this track on June 17 over 1300m, head behind Buccino with 56.5kg at $11. Fitter now and making headway. Will take plenty of beating.

runners.name: THREEZ COMPANY
runners.comment: At debut, 7th of 11 at Pukekohe on June 24 over 1200m, in the wet; 10 len behind Elite Choice with 55.5kg at $6.50. No surprise to see her in the finish.

runners.name: IN THE VINEYARD
runners.comment: Second-up. First-up after three months 4th of 10 at this track on June 17 over 970m, 3.5 len behind Smashing Sister with 53.5kg at $4.70. Previously second-up 2nd in a small field at New Plymouth Raceway on January 24 over 1200m, on rain affected going; 2.75 len behind King Of The Air with 54.5kg at $2.60. Rates highly and should be in the mix.

runners.name: BIT OF A DAGG
runners.comment: Resuming after a lengthy spell. Only one run when sat behind the speed prior to fading 8th of 10 at Te Rapa on January 30 over 1300m, on a slow track; 7 len behind Mosala carrying 57.5kg at $7.40. Looks all set for a positive return.

runners.name: BY DEFAULT
runners.comment: Resuming from a lengthy break. Only one run when 7th of 11 at New Plymouth Raceway Mdn on November 5 over 1600m, 6 len behind Shamwari with 56.5kg at $18.30. Others look better.

runners.name: ANNIE BLING
runners.comment: Last start 5th of 11 at this track on June 17 over 1300m, 3 len behind Buccino carrying 56.5kg at $30.60. Promising signs last time and is worth including.

runners.name: LOMASI
runners.comment: At debut, 7th of 12 at Matamata on June 14 over 1400m, on a slow track; 5.5 len behind Dare To Proisir carrying 55.5kg at $11.80. Can do better. Plenty of scope for improvement.

runners.name: DARE YOU
runners.comment: Last start didn't do much in the run home; 8th of 10 at Te Aroha on June 21 over 1400m, on rain affected going; 11 len behind Sugar Daddy carrying 52.5kg at $15.80. runners.name: JUANITA
runners.comment: First starter by Vanbrugh out of Itsonlymakebelieve. Third May 26 at Te Awamutu at her only trial. Favouring others until she proves herself.

runners.name: MILLENNIUM MAGIC
runners.comment: She is resuming after a six-and-a-half months break. runners.name: TREASURE HUNT
runners.comment: She is resuming after a three-and-a-half months break. On debut ninth of 11 at Te Aroha March 6 over 1150m, soft track; 5 len behind Emma Twigg with 57kg at $15.50. Will be better for the run.

runners.name: LIT SHOW
runners.comment: First-starter by Super Seth out of Ipso Facto. Hard to recommend without a market push.

runners.comment: Two-year-old colt (Ready For Moni - Jj's Jordyn) making his debut. Showed ability when he won at The Meadows in a trial on June 23 over 1609m. Profiles nicely but betting will hold the key.

runners.comment: Two-year-old gelding by Triumphant Caviar out of Crazy Smart on debut. Finished eighth at Scioto Downs in a trial on June 15 over 1609m. Betting will prove to be the best pointer

R:PNN:5:2026-07-09.comment: 1. TRY TO MAKE CENTS is racing well. Scored at Delaware Park (Dirt) two back then finished second, beaten 1.5 lengths, over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) at the latest appearance three-and-a-half weeks ago. Should camp just off the leaders. Leading contender. 3. SERGEANT CAPPS returns from a break of four-and-a-half months. Consistent performer last prep whose most recent appearance resulted in a seventh over 1609m at Aqueduct. Finds the right race to launch another bright campaign. 5. TUFF LUCK was racing well without winning, finishing in the money on three consecutive occasions, before finishing fifth last time out over 1710m at Laurel. Has the gate speed to take up a forward position. Capable of testing this field. 2. FENTON FOREVER is a promising galloper who resumes from a break. Last raced when third over 1609m at Parx (Dirt) a year ago which was the only run of that prep. Has never missed the frame when fresh from a spell in two starts. Has the pace to settle in the leading division. One of the better hopes.

runners.name: TRY TO MAKE CENTS
runners.comment: Form hard to fault. Last start 2nd in a small field at Parx on June 15 over 1609m, 1.5 len behind El Cordobes with 55.5kg at $11.60. The start before that won by 2.5 len at Delaware Park May 30 over 1710m defeating The Great Oscar with 55.5kg at $3.50. Has the ability to be in the finish.

runners.name: BRINDANO
runners.comment: At the latest run got back early 3rd in a small field at Laurel on June 20 over 1609m, 8 len behind Jolly By Golly carrying 56kg at $3.10. The start before that 2nd in a small field at Laurel on May 22 over 1710m, 5.5 len behind Preacha Meyers carrying 53.5kg at $10.20. Better off investing elsewhere.

runners.name: FENTON FOREVER
runners.comment: Back after a long spell. Only had one start last preparation tracked the speed and boxed on steadily 3rd of 7 at Parx on July 9 over 1609m, 7 len behind Light Forever with 54kg at $5.30. Won previous preparation first up scored a big 3.5 len win at Parx November 18 2024 over 1710m defeating Reelin N Dealin with 55.5kg at $3.50. Has the ability to run well.

runners.name: TUFF LUCK
runners.comment: Returns from a 38-day let-up. Most recently 5th of 8 at Laurel on May 31 over 1710m, 3.5 len behind Guaponess carrying 56kg at $3.60. The run before that 2nd in a small field at Laurel on May 14 over 1609m, 2.75 len behind El De Larry with 53.5kg at $2.50. Best form should see him measure right up.

runners.name: SERGEANT CAPPS
runners.comment: Resuming after four-and-a-half months in the paddock. Finished off last campaign seventh of 9 at Aqueduct March 1 over 1609m, 5 len behind Ambition carrying 56kg at $11.63. When resuming last campaign ran last of 11 at Ellis Park Turf July 4 2025 over 1609m, 16 len behind Lebowski with 53.5kg at $25.12. Likely to feature.

runners.name: KAZ BRIO

The run before that 4th of 7 at Aqueduct on March 20 over 1408m, 6 len behind First Blessing with 55.5kg at $11.52. Last-start winner who can measure up again.

runners.name: NOBLIGHT

Should be ready to show her best now so she is a strong chance to break through. 8. JOHN D was narrowly beaten into second when second up last start at this track (Dirt) over 320m. Will drift back but wind up late. Will be doing his best work late.

runners.comment: Third-up today. Resuming 4th of 10 at this track on May 30 over 366m, 1.0 len behind Sydney Jack carrying 57kg at $9.10. Second run from a spell 4th of 10 at this track in LR on June 18 over 366m, 2 len behind Tw Streakin Cowboy with 57kg at $75.90. Should be near his top.

runners.name: FAV STREAKIN CARTE
runners.comment: Third-up today. Resumes off a 58 day letup. First-up got back early 5th of 10 at this track on April 8 over 274m, 2.5 len behind Send Me A Dynasty Hg with 57kg at $24.40. Second run from a spell 2nd of 10 at this track on May 11 over 320m, 0.1 len behind Run Dont Walk with 57kg at $14.20. Fitter now and making headway. Will take beating.

runners.comment: Third-up today. Resuming eased back early on 8th of 10 at this track on May 30 over 366m, 5 len behind Roses After Midnight carrying 57kg at $9.70.
